assertions.reason: duplicateEnvelope parallelizes independent ID/meta work and creates recipients concurrently instead of in a sequential loop.

evidence.details: The function awaits Promise.all where incrementDocumentId()/incrementTemplateId() runs in parallel with prisma.documentMeta.create(...), removing a sequential DB waterfall.
Recipient duplication uses await Promise.all(envelope.recipients.map(async (recipient) => prisma.recipient.create(...))), so recipients are created concurrently rather than via a sequential loop.

assertions.reason: The envelope edit loader uses Promise.all for the independent post-token fetches; the later organisation email query remains sequential because it depends on the envelope result.

evidence.details: After token verification, the loader awaits Promise.all([getTeamSettings(...), getEditorEnvelopeById(...)]), so the settings and envelope fetches run concurrently.
The subsequent prisma.organisationEmail.findMany query uses envelope.team.organisationId, showing why it is dependent on the envelope fetch and therefore not included in the Promise.all block.

summary: Both assigned assertions pass based on read-only inspection of the assigned files. duplicateEnvelope uses Promise.all for independent metadata/ID work and recipient creation, and the envelope edit loader parallelizes the independent settings/envelope fetches while keeping the dependent organisation email lookup sequential.
